description.en: A puffed North Indian bread of yogurt-fermented dough, deep-fried until it balloons into a golden, hollow pocket. Its tang comes from a long yogurt rest rather than commercial yeast, and it is eaten hot alongside spiced chickpea curry as chole bhature.
description.de: Ein aufgeplustertes nordindisches Brot aus joghurtfermentiertem Teig, das beim Frittieren zu einer goldenen, hohlen Blase aufgeht. Seine Säure stammt von einer langen Joghurtrast statt von Hefe; es wird heiß zum würzigen Kichererbsen-Curry als Chole Bhature gegessen.
description.es: Un pan inflado del norte de India hecho con masa fermentada en yogur, frito hasta convertirse en una bolsa dorada y hueca. Su acidez viene de un largo reposo con yogur y no de levadura comercial, y se come caliente junto al curry de garbanzos como chole bhature.
description.fr: Un pain gonflé du nord de l'Inde à base de pâte fermentée au yaourt, frit jusqu'à former une poche dorée et creuse. Son acidité vient d'un long repos au yaourt plutôt que de la levure du commerce, et il se mange chaud avec un curry de pois chiches en chole bhature.

origin.note: The classic partner of chole (spiced chickpeas); yeast is a modern shortcut, the traditional rise comes only from fermented yogurt and a long rest.

ingredients.note: for deep-frying; amount absorbed is estimated

keeping.note: Eats best minutes after frying while still puffed; deflates and toughens once cold. Shaped raw dough balls freeze well before frying.

method.title: Mix dough
method.summary: Whisk flour, semolina and salt, work in yogurt and oil, stir in baking powder and soda, then add warm water gradually to a soft, elastic dough; knead about 5 minutes.

method.title: Ferment
method.summary: Cover and let rest 2-4 hours in a warm place until noticeably softened.

method.title: Divide and shape
method.summary: Divide into 8 balls, oil each lightly, and roll into 6-7 inch ovals.

method.title: Heat the oil
method.summary: Heat oil in a deep pot to about 190°C, until a small piece of dough rises to the surface without browning right away.

method.title: Fry
method.summary: Slide a bhatura into the oil, press gently with a spatula as it rises so it puffs fully, then flip and fry the second side until golden.

method.title: Drain and serve
method.summary: Lift out, drain briefly, and serve piping hot with chickpea curry, sliced onion and pickle.

source.adaptation: Used baking soda/powder rather than fruit-salt (Eno) to fit the taxonomy; frying-oil absorption estimated per convention; salt increased slightly from the source's very light 1/4 tsp.
